zh.Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭藍納可爾格拉斯哥
zh.Blantyre, Lanarkshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭藍納可爾布蘭太爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Isabella Normond Wilson於1503年出生在英國蘇格蘭格拉斯哥時，她的父親William Normond33歲，母親Andrew Thompson30歲。她於1519年與Sir Andrew M Thompson在蘇格蘭藍納可爾格拉斯哥結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1571年8月28日在英國蘇格蘭享年68歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland: 苏格兰藍納可爾格拉斯哥
zh-hans.Blantyre, Lanarkshire, Scotland: 苏格兰藍納可爾布蘭太爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Isabella Normond Wilson于1503年出生在英国苏格兰格拉斯哥时，她的父亲William Normond33岁，母亲Andrew Thompson30岁。她于1519年与Sir Andrew M Thompson在苏格兰藍納可爾格拉斯哥结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1571年8月28日在英国苏格兰享年68岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Normond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Andre, Laurent, Marcel, Pierre, Amie, Donat, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean Noel, Jean-Michel.
French and Breton (mainly Finistère; also Le Normand): habitational name for someone from Normandy, a region in northern France (see Norman ). Compare Lenormand .
Scottish (Fife): variant of   Norman  .

variants.name: Norman
meanings: English, Irish (Dublin and Cork), and Scottish: ethnic or habitational name applied either to a Scandinavian or to someone from Normandy in northern France. The Scandinavian adventurers of the Dark Ages called themselves northmenn ‘men from the North’. Before 1066, Scandinavian settlers in England were already fairly readily absorbed, and Northman and Normann came to be used as bynames and later as personal names, even among the Saxon inhabitants. The term gained a new use from 1066 onward, when England was settled by invaders from Normandy, who were likewise of Scandinavian origin but by now largely integrated with the native population and speaking a Romance language, retaining only their original ancient Germanic name.
English: from the Middle English personal name Norman (recorded in the late Old English period as Northman), derived from northman ‘northerner’.
Americanized form of German   Normann  .

variants.name: Normandeau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel.
French: from a diminutive of   Normand  .

variants.name: Lormand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Eustache.
Altered form of French   Normand  .
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Le Lormand in the Pyrénées-Atlantique.

variants.name: Norment
meanings: Scottish: probably a variant of   Normand  .

variants.name: Lenormand
meanings: French; Breton (Côtes-d'Armor and Morbihan; mainly Le Normand): variant of Normand , with the French masculine definite article le.

variants.name: Normandin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Alain, Serge, Adrien, Andre, Aurore, Celine, Clovis, Emile, Gaetan, Gilles, Laurier.
French: from a diminutive of   Normand  . Compare   Normandy  .

nameMeanings.name: Wilson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Will + patronymic -son ‘son of Will’. Will was a very common medieval short form of William . This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Willison
meanings: English: variant of   Wilson  .

variants.name: Will
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Will, a short form of William . This surname is now commonly found in Scotland, in Aberdeenshire, Angus, and Kincardineshire.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a spring or stream, from Middle English wel, wel(l)e, wille, in a southwestern dialect form. This surname is found predominantly in the south and southwestern parts of the country. Compare Well 1.
English: nickname from Middle English iwil, wil(l)e ‘agreeable, pleasing’.

variants.name: Willson
meanings: English (eastern): variant of   Wilson  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Isabella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Isabella Normond Wilson was born in 1503, in Glasgow, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, William Normond, was 33 and her mother, Andrew Thompson, was 30. She married Sir Andrew M Thompson in 1519, in Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 28 August 1571, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 68, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Isabella Normond Wilson est née en 1503, à Glasgow, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Normond, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Andrew Thompson, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Andrew M Thompson en 1519, à Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 août 1571, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Isabella Normond Wilson 1503 in Glasgow, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Normond 33 und ihre Mutter Andrew Thompson 30. Sie hat 1519 in Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland Sir Andrew M Thompson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. August 1571 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabella Normond Wilson è nata il nel 1503 nel Glasgow, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Normond, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Andrew Thompson, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Sir Andrew M Thompson nel 1519, nel Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 28 agosto 1571, nel Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Isabella Normond Wilsonが1503年にイギリススコットランドグラスゴウで生まれた時，父William Normondは33歳，母Andrew Thompsonは30歳であった。1519年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアグラスゴウでSir Andrew M Thompsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1571年8月28日にイギリススコットランドで68歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabella Normond Wilson(이)가 1503년 영국 스코틀랜드 글라스고에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Normond(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Andrew Thompson(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1519년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 글라스고에서 Sir Andrew M Thompson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1571년 8월 28일 영국 스코틀랜드에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabella Normond Wilson nasceu em 1503, em Glasgow, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Normond, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Andrew Thompson, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Andrew M Thompson em 1519, em Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de agosto de 1571, em Escócia, United Kingdom, com 68 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Isabella Normond Wilson родилась в 1503 г. в местности гласгоу, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Normond было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Andrew Thompson, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Andrew M Thompson в 1519 г. в местности гласгоу, ланаркшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 28 августа 1571 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Isabella Normond Wilson nació en 1503, en Glasgow, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, William Normond, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Andrew Thompson, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Sir Andrew M Thompson en 1519, en Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 28 de agosto de 1571, en Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
